summary: Buying a home in India? This episode is your one-stop playbook, from real budgets in Mumbai/Thane to loan readiness, resale vs developer due-diligence, and the hidden costs that turn a ₹2 Cr home into ₹2.4–2.5 Cr. We also cover red flags like “proposed development”, show-apartment traps, APF checks, CIBIL/ITR, broker value, and the age-old question: buy now or wait?

seo.title: Hidden Home-Buying Costs & Smart Buying Guide (India) | घर खरीदने से पहले ज़रूर देखें
seo.description: Buying a home in India? This episode is your one-stop playbook, from real budgets in Mumbai/Thane to loan readiness, resale vs developer due-diligence, and

takeaways: A ₹2 crore home rarely costs ₹2 crore: stamp duty near 6%, GST near 5%, registration, processing fees and interiors can push the real outlay to ₹2.4 to 2.5 crore.
Get loan-ready before you house-hunt, because lenders read your ITR, CIBIL, bank statements and savings, so closing liabilities and combining incomes lifts your eligibility.
If HDFC, ICICI or SBI will not lend against a project, treat it as a warning sign, since big-bank APF approval doubles as built-in due diligence.
Look past the show apartment: verify OC, title chain, water and gas, watch for 'proposed development' and 'bang on main road' traps, and see the property in both daylight and at night.
Interiors are the cost most buyers forget, often ₹10 to 30 lakh, so build them into the budget from day one rather than after possession.
